maml:description.maml:para: Get an access key.
Gets all cloud access keys available to you or returns a specific key by its UID.
Your access key's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your access key version's ID.
Your access key's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your access key version's ID.
Get the status of an access key.
Gets the current status and other details for a request to create a new access key.
Your status request's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your status request's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Get an access key version.
Gets all versions of a cloud access key or returns a specific version.
Your access key's ID.
Your access key's version.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your access key's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your access key's version.
Get the status of an access key version.
Gets the current status and other details for a request to create a new access key version.
Your status request's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your status request's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Look up properties.
Gets information about all of the Property Manager properties that use a specific version of an access key.
Your access key's ID.
Your access key's version.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your access key's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your access key's version.
Perform a property lookup request asynchronously.

Provides functionality similar to the `Get-CloudAccessKeyVersionProperties` operation, but accesses the data asynchronously after running the `New-CloudAccessLookup` operation to obtain the `lookupId`.
Your property lookup's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Your property lookup's ID.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Create an access key.
Creates a new access key. An access key's name needs to be unique within a user account. Once you create an access key, it's available on both the staging and production Akamai networks.
The new name for the access key. The `accessKeyName` value must be unique for each key within an account.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

Additional type of the deployment network that the access key will be deployed to. Include CHINA_CDN to make the access key available in Akamai's China CDN. Include RUSSIA_CDN to make the access key available in Akamai's Russia CDN. If you're using a Standard TLS certificate for security, RUSSIA_CDN is included by default, so you don't need to include it here. If you're using an Enhanced TLS certificate, you'll need to include both to access these CDNs.

The type of cloud provider signing process used to authenticate API requests. Use `AOS4_HMAC_SHA256` for Akamai Object Storage (https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/object-storage), `AWS4_HMAC_SHA256` for Amazon Web Services, `GOOG4_HMAC_SHA256` for Google Cloud Services in interoperability mode, `AVM_CLOUDINARY` for Video Manager by Cloudinary, or `VP_QUEUE_IT` for Visitor Prioritization by Queue-it.
The ID that's combined with the `-CloudSecretAccessKey` to sign API requests. This is the access key ID (AWS) or access ID (GCS) you get from your cloud provider.
A value that's combined with the `-CloudAccessKeyID` to sign API requests. This is the secret access key (AWS) or secret (GCS) you get from your cloud provider.
The unique identifier for the contract assigned to the access key.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The unique identifier assigned to the access control group (doc:concepts)assigned to the access key.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

The API deploys the access key to this secure network. STANDARD_TLS deploys the access key to Akamai's standard secure network. This is non-PCI compliant. ENHANCED_TLS deploys the access key to Akamai's more secure network which is PCI compliant.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The new name for the access key. The `accessKeyName` value must be unique for each key within an account.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

Additional type of the deployment network that the access key will be deployed to. Include CHINA_CDN to make the access key available in Akamai's China CDN. Include RUSSIA_CDN to make the access key available in Akamai's Russia CDN. If you're using a Standard TLS certificate for security, RUSSIA_CDN is included by default, so you don't need to include it here. If you're using an Enhanced TLS certificate, you'll need to include both to access these CDNs.

The type of cloud provider signing process used to authenticate API requests. Use `AOS4_HMAC_SHA256` for Akamai Object Storage (https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/object-storage), `AWS4_HMAC_SHA256` for Amazon Web Services, `GOOG4_HMAC_SHA256` for Google Cloud Services in interoperability mode, `AVM_CLOUDINARY` for Video Manager by Cloudinary, or `VP_QUEUE_IT` for Visitor Prioritization by Queue-it.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
The ID that's combined with the `-CloudSecretAccessKey` to sign API requests. This is the access key ID (AWS) or access ID (GCS) you get from your cloud provider.
A value that's combined with the `-CloudAccessKeyID` to sign API requests. This is the secret access key (AWS) or secret (GCS) you get from your cloud provider.
The unique identifier for the contract assigned to the access key.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The unique identifier assigned to the access control group (doc:concepts)assigned to the access key.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

The API deploys the access key to this secure network. STANDARD_TLS deploys the access key to Akamai's standard secure network. This is non-PCI compliant. ENHANCED_TLS deploys the access key to Akamai's more secure network which is PCI compliant.
Create an access key version.

Rotates an access key to a new version. > Note: You need to do this only if your cloud provider credentials have changed, for example, because they've expired or been compromised. Only two versions of an access key can exist and be active at the same time. If you have two access key versions, you have to run the `Remove-CloudAccessKeyVersion` operation before you can create another version.
Your access key's ID.
The ID that's combined with the `-CloudSecretAccessKey` to sign API requests. This is the access key ID (AWS) or access ID (GCS) you get from your cloud provider.
A value that's combined with the `-CloudAccessKeyID` to sign API requests. This is the secret access key (AWS) or secret (GCS) you get from your cloud provider.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your access key's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The ID that's combined with the `-CloudSecretAccessKey` to sign API requests. This is the access key ID (AWS) or access ID (GCS) you get from your cloud provider.
A value that's combined with the `-CloudAccessKeyID` to sign API requests. This is the secret access key (AWS) or secret (GCS) you get from your cloud provider.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Search properties for access keys.
Asynchronously searches for properties using a version of a cloud access key.
Your access key's ID.
Your access key's version.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your access key's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your access key's version.
Delete an access key.
Deletes a specific access key.
Your access key's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your access key's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Delete an access key version.
Deletes a specific version of an access key. > Note: You can't delete an access key version if it is still in use.
Your access key's ID.
Your access key's version.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your access key's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your access key's version.
Update an access key.
Updates an access key's name.
Your access key's ID.
The new name for the access key. The `accessKeyName` value must be unique for each key within an account.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The new name for the access key. The `accessKeyName` value must be unique for each key within an account.
Your access key's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
